mathematical-notations.md.summary: Symbol glossary / pronunciation guide; no worked examples by design.

set-theory.md.summary: Exemplary on the hard proofs (Cantor diagonal, Cantor theorem, Kuratowski, Q/Z enumerations, double-inclusion); several clear-but-terse operations rated adequate; no thin/missing (injection/surjection detail lives on functions-relations).

inventory.upgrade: add a worked identity proof, e.g. (A∪B)\B = A∖B by element-chasing
prove the law (element-chasing), not just compute one instance
add a concrete 5-into-3 example showing ceil(5/3)=2
worked check of a concrete map (defers detail to functions-relations)

number-systems.md.summary: Strong on the narrative + repeating-decimal conversions + containment; the FORMAL CONSTRUCTIONS (Dedekind cuts, Cauchy sequences, pair-and-quotient for Z, von Neumann naturals, Q/R countability, FTA) are stated but never walked -> 7 thin items.

inventory.upgrade: add a long-division walkthrough showing remainders must recur
add substitution check that sqrt2 solves x^2-2=0; maybe cbrt2 edge case
add one cross-mult verification (1/2 ~ 2/4)
add a nontrivial product e.g. (2,3)(1,1)=(-1,5)
tighten the link to the full Real Analysis proof
walk the first ~8 rationals in zigzag order, showing the skip-duplicates rule
sketch the diagonal on a small 3x3 grid so the 'no list works' idea is concrete
build 0..4 step-by-step; verify 2∈4 encodes 2<4
verify (5,3)~(2,0) via 5+0=3+2; contrast a non-equivalent pair
build the sqrt2 cut: list rationals in A and not in A; interpret as 'everything up to sqrt2'
walk (1,1.4,1.41,1.414,...) -> sqrt2, check consecutive terms shrink
solve x^2-2x+2=0 -> 1±i and verify 1+i by substitution

functions-relations.md.summary: Solid on function properties (injective/surjective/inverse/composition/difference quotient) and excellent on the Schroder-Bernstein proof; relation properties are defined but rarely proved, and fibers/kernel + combined bijective proof lack worked examples.

inventory.concept: image & preimage of a set (+ asymmetry)

inventory.upgrade: add a positive example verifying a relation IS a function
derive [0],[1],[2] of mod-3 and show they partition Z
prove injective + surjective, not just assert 'each integer hit once'
compute join/meet of {2,3} in a divisibility poset

inventory.concept: fibers and kernel of a function
inventory.upgrade: compute fibers of x^2 (over 4 -> {-2,2}, over -1 -> empty); show they partition R
one worked proof that a specific f is BOTH injective and surjective, then its inverse

inventory.concept: checking reflexive/symmetric/transitive on a concrete relation
inventory.upgrade: prove 'divides' on Z+ is a partial order, checking each property
prove 'same age' (or congruence mod n) is reflexive+symmetric+transitive

euclidean-geometry.md.summary: HIGHEST-PRIORITY foundation target: strong vocabulary/taxonomy but a definition-catalog with ~10 thin/missing worked examples -- most theorems are stated and never demonstrated on concrete numbers.

inventory.upgrade: add a compute-the-inscribed-angle example + harness entry
add WE-5 motivation and harness entries
add cone = 1/3 cylinder worked comparison
worked: angle 42 deg -> complement 48 deg with check
pentagon: (5-2)*180=540, each interior 108, with steps
given 55 and 70 deg find the third with steps + check
worked: equal alternate-interior angles => prove lines parallel
worked SSS on two 3-4-5 triangles, conclude corresponding angles equal
worked AA similarity with a side-ratio computation
parallelogram: given one angle, find the rest with check
translate a point by a vector; verify distance preserved
dilate a 3-4-5 triangle by 2 -> 6-8-10; shape preserved
regular hexagon: 6 axes + 60-deg rotational symmetry, verified

permutation.md.summary: Excellent foundational + advanced walkthroughs (counting tree, MISSISSIPPI, circular, bracelets); the two core formulas (P(n,r), n^r) are adequate but lean on diagram intuition rather than re-deriving from counting.

inventory.upgrade: tie 5x4x3 = 5!/2! by showing the unused 2! cancels
enumerate a toy case (2 slots from {A,B,C}=9) before generalizing

propositional-logic-zeroth-order-logic.m.summary: Strong truth-table + core-concept coverage; the PROOF-TECHNIQUE walkthroughs hand-wave the crux -- the sqrt2 contradiction skips 'a^2 even => a even', and the induction step under-explains the inductive-hypothesis substitution.

inventory.upgrade: add a two-truth-tables side-by-side verification
worked De Morgan on ¬((P∧Q)∨R) step-by-step
side-by-side modus ponens vs affirming-the-consequent with tables

inventory.concept: rules of inference (applying them in a derivation)
